theme: The best interactions in this office are invisible ŌĆö let's fix that
visual_observations: Desktop (1280x800): Office floor renders cleanly with grid background and purple border. Bloberto center-top with purple glow desk, CVO label. Luna top-left with her crescent moon avatar and colorful block desk screen. Carlos and Maya working at middle desks with their screen SVGs visible. Dave and Sofia at bottom desks ŌĆö Sofia shows red ! error badge. VACANT desk bottom-right: completely dead, just the text 'VACANT' against empty grid. Conference table bottom-left renders as a circle with 6 orbit chair-dots, currently empty, ambient label reads 'everyone is at their desk'. Right wall: COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT cup, green cactus, the amber/orange window rectangle (sky gradient), small green plant, ŌśĆ’ĖÅ weather icon. Bottom: Entrance door labeled ENTRANCE. Shortcut toast renders inside the office floor boundary on desktop (correct). Header greeting: 'Good evening, LEGEND ­¤öź ┬Ę Sunday, Mar 8' with ­¤öź Crushing It vibe badge. Footer shows vibe-specific tagline with a faint '1/9' counter nobody sees. Mobile (375x812): Same office renders but keyboard shortcut bar floats BELOW the office card in document body ŌĆö confirmed still broken from review #52. Conference table chairs readable at this scale. VACANT desk even more prominent as dead corner on mobile.

ideas.title: The Footer Tagline Easter Egg Is Invisible
ideas.description: The footer tagline is already clickable and cycles through 9 bonus quotes ŌĆö one of the best hidden features in the app. But the only affordance is a browser title tooltip that says 'tap for another thought' and a `1/9` counter at 0.3 opacity that blends into nothing. On mobile there is zero affordance; title tooltips don't fire on touch. Fix: (1) Bump the counter opacity to 0.55 and give it a `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` style so it reads clearly as `1/9`. (2) Add a tiny Ō£” sparkle icon immediately before the opening quote mark ŌĆö styled as `cursor: pointer` with a `@keyframes twinkle` that pulses twice on first load (localStorage flag). (3) On tap/click, add a brief CSS text shimmer (`@keyframes quote-swap`: opacity 0ŌåÆ1 + blur 4pxŌåÆ0, 0.25s ease). WHY: This mechanic is already built and delightful. People just don't know it exists. One sparkle and a readable counter turns a secret into a discoverable moment.

ideas.title: VACANT Desk Posts a Vibe-Based Job Ad
ideas.description: The VACANT desk is the only dead corner in the entire office floor ŌĆö no character, no screen SVG, no interaction, just the word VACANT against empty grid. Add a hover state that pops up a micro job-posting card. No backend needed; build a static VACANT_ADS map keyed by vibeKey: `crushing: { title: 'Staff Wizard', req: 'Must maintain velocity without asking what velocity means' }`, `on-fire: { title: 'URGENT: Firefighter', req: 'Start Monday. Like, this Monday.' }`, `slow-day: { title: 'Chaos Wrangler', req: 'Previous experience with vibe collapse preferred' }`, `in-flow: { title: 'Flow Keeper', req: 'Do not disrupt the rhythm. That is the job.' }`, `after-hours: { title: 'Night Watch', req: 'The servers need company.' }`. Render the ad as a `.vacant-job-card` positioned above the desk on hover ŌĆö styled like a sticky-note with role title, one funny requirement, and a winking ­¤æĆ CTA. WHY: Every other desk tells a story. The VACANT desk tells nothing. A vibe-reactive job ad turns the emptiest spot on the floor into the funniest.

ideas.title: Desk Screens React to Task Type, Not Just Errors
ideas.description: Each worker has a custom desk screen SVG that is beautiful and totally static. The `_error` screen variants already exist and auto-apply on `status === error`. But when Carlos is deploying (task matches Ship tag), his screen still shows calm blue bars. When Maya is fixing a bug, her wireframe screen is serene. The `getTaskTags()` function already parses the task string ŌĆö that data just isn't wired to the desk screen SVG selector. Fix: In Office.jsx, derive a `screenVariant` from the worker's task tags: `error` (existing) ŌåÆ error SVG; `ship/deploy` ŌåÆ add a `_deploy` variant (green/pulse, terminal-like); `bug` (non-error status) ŌåÆ a `_debug` variant (amber, flickery). Pass `screenVariant` to the desk SVG selector alongside `worker.id`. Only 2-3 new SVG variants needed; the wiring logic is 10 lines. WHY: The desk screens are the most visible evidence of what each person is doing right now. When they never change, they become wallpaper.

ideas.title: The Honorific Should Type Itself In
ideas.description: The header greeting includes a vibe-reactive honorific: 'LEGEND ­¤öź' (crushing), 'captain ­¤Ü©' (on-fire), 'boss' (in-flow), 'chief... you still there? ­¤śÉ' (slow-day), 'night owl ­¤īÖ' (after-hours). These are among the most personality-rich words on the page. When vibe changes and the honorific swaps, it does so instantly ŌĆö a cold text replacement. Fix: Wrap the honorific in a `<span key={teamVibe.key} className='honorific'>`. Add `@keyframes honorific-in { from { opacity: 0; filter: blur(6px); letter-spacing: 0.3em; } to { opacity: 1; filter: blur(0); letter-spacing: normal; } }` with a 0.4s ease-out. The `key` change triggers React to remount the span, firing the animation on every vibe change. No JS logic changes needed. WHY: The honorific is the most personal text in the app. When it changes, it should feel like Bloberto is re-evaluating you. That moment deserves drama.

ideas.title: The Wall Clock Has No Opinion About the Time
ideas.description: The WallClock component renders a real-time SVG analog clock that updates every 60 seconds. It already changes stroke and fill colors by vibe. It literally knows what time it is and what the vibe is ŌĆö and says nothing. Add a `data-tooltip` attribute (using the existing CSS tooltip system) with a vibe-keyed comment: `crushing: 'Peak hours. Nobody's watching the clock except the clock.'`, `on-fire: 'Time has no meaning during an incident.'`, `slow-day: 'You've checked this three times in the last hour.'`, `in-flow: 'Don't look. It breaks the flow.'`, `after-hours: 'Still here? So am I. I'm a clock. That's fine for me.'`. Also render the digital time string (h:mm AM/PM) inside the tooltip. WHY: A clock that knows the vibe and has a dry comment about the time is a micro-interaction that rewards the curious user who wonders what that tiny SVG is.

ideas.title: Clicking a Character Has No Affordance
ideas.description: Clicking any non-manager character in the Office floor triggers a 700ms ping animation AND navigates to the Dashboard tab with that worker's card focused and scrolled into view. This is the most useful interaction in the entire office floor. Almost nobody discovers it. The characters show `cursor: pointer` (good) but there is no hover tooltip, no visual hint, and no label suggesting that clicking does anything. Fix: (1) Add `data-tooltip='click to inspect {firstName}'` to each clickable character `<div>`. (2) On hover (desktop), show a tiny `ŌåŚ Dashboard` pill that fades in below the character name using `.char__name::after` ŌĆö appear after 400ms hover delay so it doesn't flash constantly. (3) On mobile, the `pinnedCard` mechanism already shows a hover-card on tap-first ŌĆö make the 'Ōä╣’ĖÅ' icon on that pinned card explicitly say 'View in Dashboard'. WHY: Character-click-to-navigate is the bridge between the visual office and the data dashboard. Right now it's a secret. It shouldn't be.

ideas.title: Conference Table Label Changes Silently
ideas.description: The `.conf-ambient-label` already shows 5 vibe-specific messages: 'everyone is at their desk' (crushing), 'no meetings. fix it.' (on-fire), 'reserve the table' (in-flow), 'open office hours' (slow-day), 'locked up' (after-hours). These are excellent micro-copy. But when vibe changes and the label swaps, it does so with a hard cut ŌĆö no animation, no emphasis. The joke lands but without a rimshot. Fix: Add `key={vibe}` to the `<div className='conf-ambient-label'>` element. Add a CSS entry animation: `@keyframes label-arrive { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(4px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } }` applied to `.conf-ambient-label` with `animation: label-arrive 0.4s ease`. The key swap triggers remount on vibe change, firing the animation. No JS logic changes. WHY: 'no meetings. fix it.' is three words of comedy gold. Right now it materializes quietly. It deserves a tiny entrance.

ideas.title: The Entrance Door Gets Ambient Visitor Events
ideas.description: Between real worker arrivals and departures, the Entrance door sits static and dark ŌĆö just a labeled door at the bottom of the floor. Real offices have ambient interruptions: deliveries, wrong-floor visitors, mysterious stakeholders. Add a pure front-end ambient event system: a `useEffect` with a randomized interval (8ŌĆō15 minutes, seeded differently per browser session) fires a `visitorEvent` state containing a randomly chosen visitor from a static VISITORS array: `[{ emoji: '­¤ō”', label: 'Delivery' }, { emoji: '­¤ŹĢ', label: 'Lunch order' }, { emoji: '­¤ōŗ', label: 'The Board' }, { emoji: '­¤”å', label: 'Wrong floor (sorry)' }, { emoji: '­¤¦æŌĆŹ­¤Æ╗', label: 'External consultant' }, { emoji: '­¤ī«', label: 'Catering arrived' }]`. When triggered: the door plays the existing arrive animation, a `.visitor-badge` label slides up (same as the proposed door name badge from review #52, but for visitors), and auto-dismisses after 3 seconds. The worker roster is never touched. WHY: The door is the office's threshold. Making it react to imaginary visitors ŌĆö at quiet random intervals ŌĆö turns a static floor into a breathing space. You never know when The Board is dropping by.
